items.minimax_notes: Hit max_tokens=3500 (finish_reason=length). Scratch reasoning correctly derived all 4 answers (10^25 FLOP + 72hr; 30-day/6-year/12-20M GBP UK trio; >10^26 and >3x10^25 FLOP, critical-harms narrowing; model-eval+red-team logs with 6yr/18mo/event-level retention split). But the response devolved into self-referential meta-commentary about how to format the FINAL block and was truncated literally at '[FINAL]' with zero formatted answers delivered. v2's larger token budget did not help because the model still got stuck in a reasoning loop on this prompt.

items.minimax_notes: All 4 correct. Three hardware changes with before/after values (128->192 GB, 900 GB/s->1.3 TB/s, 580 GB/s->1.0 TB/s). Q2 computed both speedups (3.25x, 1.94x) and reconciled with document's rounded 3.2x/1.9x. Q3: >850 GB/s -> 760 GB/s. Q4: batch >24, intra-node bandwidth saturates + all-to-all amortizes.
items.dsv4_notes: All 4 correct in 273 tokens. Used document's 3.2x/1.9x rounded values rather than computing 3.25x/1.94x; rubric accepts either.

summary.overall_takeaway: v2's fixes (system prompt putting [FINAL] block first + max_tokens=3500) recovered E2 fully (5->10) and recovered E1 most of the way (7->8.5; lost 1.5 pts to a Q4 max-p99 selection error, not truncation). E5 remained pathological even with 3500 tokens: MiniMax got stuck in a self-referential meta-loop about formatting and emitted no FINAL block, identical 5/10 score to v1. E3/E4/E6 were already 10/10 in v1 and stayed there. DSV4 unchanged at perfect 10s across all six items -- its terse 161-309 token style was sufficient for every 4-part question. Net: MiniMax +1.09 on longctx avg, DSV4 still wins category outright at 10.0 vs 8.92. The remaining gap is now mostly E5's failure mode (reasoning meta-loop, not pure truncation) plus the Q4 max-vs-first-impact selection on E1. To close further: add explicit instruction to not deliberate about formatting in the system prompt, or use stop sequences to cap scratchpad length.
